text: Seed-robustness check (e-0044): the DOMAIN-RECOVERY claim of a-0001/a-0002 is stable, but the exact partition the bridge analysis builds on is not. Re-running Leiden (ModularityVertexPartition, 10 iterations, the exact LCC cluster.py uses: 92,594 nodes/308,095 edges) under 7 seeds {0,1,7,42,123,2024,31337} gives NMI-vs-Mathlib-module = 0.2847 +/- 0.0025 — essentially constant, anchoring to a-0002's 0.307 — and tight modularity 0.618-0.627. So 'unsupervised clustering recovers named domains' is genuinely seed-robust, not a single-seed artifact. HOWEVER the fine partition is only moderately reproducible: cluster COUNT swings 93-223 (mean 140.6 +/- 37.3) and pairwise run-to-run ARI averages just 0.465 (NMI 0.588). The canonical stored partition (138 clusters) is itself only moderately matched by fresh reruns (ARI 0.42-0.62), and a fresh seed-42 Leiden run yields 223 clusters not 138, so the stored clusters.json reflects extra processing, not a bare reproducible run. Implication: aggregate module-level domain structure is robust, but cluster-IDENTITY-dependent downstream claims (the specific 34 bridge theorems, the exact 138 clusters, SetTheory-as-hub-by-cluster-id in a-0010) rest on one particular partition that would shift across seeds. Caveat: ModularityVertexPartition has no resolution parameter, so the count variation is intrinsic Leiden stochasticity.
